proposed_relations.description: Future bus lanes on 34th Street discussed at Transitway CAC meeting

evidence_refs.source_quote: Consideration needs to be given to the snow removal process in the future bus lanes.
Clarify whether all buses operating on 34 th Street are allowed to use the bus lanes.

rationale: Direct evidence from CAC meeting minutes: discussion of snow removal in future bus lanes and clarifying which buses may use the lanes are part of the 34th Street Transitway concept planning.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Consideration needs to be given to the snow removal process in the future bus lanes.
Clarify whether all buses operating on 34 th Street are allowed to use the bus lanes.

quarantine.note: Neither cited block mentions 'Transitway'. p001_c0015 says 'future bus lanes' and p002_c0001 says 'bus lanes' on '34 th Street' but neither establishes the connection to the Transitway project. Co-occurrence in a Transitway CAC meeting document is not sufficient — the block text itself must state the relation.

proposed_relations.description: The proposed Transitway design will be presented at the next CAC meeting

evidence_refs.source_quote: The proposed design will be presented at the next CAC meeting.

rationale: The proposed design is for the 34th Street Transitway project, and the CAC is the Transitway's Community Advisory Committee.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The proposed design will be presented at the next CAC meeting.

quarantine.note: The cited block p006_c0001 says 'The proposed design will be presented at the next CAC meeting.' but never mentions '34th Street Transitway' or any project name. The connection between the claim and the project is purely document-level co-occurrence — the document happens to be about the Transitway CAC. Per instructions, co-occurrence in a document is not a relation; the cited block must state the connection. The evidence quote does not tie 'proposed design' to the Transitway project.
